policy.default: Every canonical key without a production callsite or reviewed dynamic family is BLOCKED_CONTEXT.
policy.proposalRule: A reserved key proposal records its concept, UI role, user-visible consequence, rationale, concrete evidence, and current sourceContextHash. English and Chinese text alone are not sufficient evidence.
policy.approvalRule: Proposals remain BLOCKED_CONTEXT until the exact current pilot or full-catalog scope is approved in an ignored local Markdown confirmation. Reviewer identity, date, and decision are never written here.
policy.hashRule: sourceContextHash pins the canonical English, Chinese, runtime/dynamic evidence, and review-policy digest. Any source or policy change invalidates the proposal before local confirmation.

messages.concept: Reserved Ant Design Pro preview action that downloads the currently previewed page into a local development project.
messages.consequence: Must describe downloading the current page into a local project; it must not imply downloading an LCA data block or exporting a data set.

evidence.rationale: Both canonical locales describe the same page-to-local-project preview action.
The key is intentionally retained in the non-runtime activation-entry candidate artifact and has no current production callsite.

messages.rationale: The proposal preserves the upstream preview-tool meaning while explicitly excluding TianGong block and data-export meanings; full-catalog review must confirm it before any future reuse.

messages.concept: Section heading for the main-content width control.
messages.consequence: Must preserve the exact layout, appearance, accessibility, or developer-configuration effect and must not imply a persisted end-user preference when the panel only edits runtime layout settings.

evidence.reference: config/defaultSettings.ts Settings and config/config.ts layout integration
evidence.rationale: The active ProLayout configuration defines contentWidth, fixedHeader, fixSiderbar, colorWeak, theme, and layout values corresponding to this retained settings family.
The application still mounts the Pro Components SettingDrawer in development, which establishes the appearance-panel consequence even though these legacy message IDs are not selected literally.

messages.concept: Compatibility success notification for a TIDAS package whose export has completed successfully.
messages.consequence: Must mean that package creation completed and must not be shown merely when the asynchronous export task was submitted or when a completed file was downloaded.

evidence.rationale: The current export is asynchronous: submission, package completion, and download are separate user-visible events.

messages.rationale: No literal production callsite selects this older success copy; the proposal fixes its meaning to export completion so it cannot collapse the current asynchronous stages.

messages.concept: Plural count of user-owned records that conflict with records already present during TIDAS import.

evidence.rationale: The active import surface exposes the same backend count and explains that existing user data conflicts with incoming records.

messages.rationale: The retained key appears to be compatibility copy for an older count presentation; product review must confirm whether it may return.

messages.concept: Plural count of open-data records skipped automatically during TIDAS import.

messages.rationale: The retained key appears to be compatibility copy for an older plural count; product review must confirm the UI role.

messages.rationale: No literal production reference currently selects this retained issue label; backend mapping must be confirmed.

messages.rationale: No literal production reference currently maps this compatibility label to a backend issue code; full-catalog product/TIDAS review must confirm that mapping.

messages.rationale: No current production callsite proves the backend issue-code mapping, so product/TIDAS review remains required.

messages.rationale: No literal production reference currently maps this compatibility key to a report code.

evidence.rationale: The source pair defines a generic fallback, and the report model confirms that detailed backend messages and context remain available separately.

messages.rationale: No literal production reference currently maps this compatibility key to a backend issue code; full-catalog product/TIDAS review must confirm the mapping.

messages.concept: Compatibility detail value identifying a task as a TIDAS package export.
messages.consequence: Must identify export rather than import, LCA calculation, or review submission and must retain the TIDAS package domain.

evidence.rationale: The active task center classifies package tasks by tidas_package_export and exposes a TIDAS export filter label.

messages.rationale: The retained detail key is not rendered by the current task detail grid; its meaning is pinned to the active export task kind for later compatibility use.

messages.concept: Compatibility detail message stating that a completed TIDAS export package is available for download.
messages.consequence: Must be used only when package creation completed and a download is available; it must not describe mere task submission or a completed download.

evidence.rationale: The active task card shows the download control only for a completed TIDAS export task.

messages.rationale: The retained detail sentence has no current literal callsite; the proposal binds it to the completed-export state and available download action.

messages.concept: Compatibility detail value identifying a task as a TIDAS package import.
messages.consequence: Must identify import rather than export, LCA calculation, or review submission and must retain the TIDAS package domain.

evidence.rationale: The active task center classifies package tasks by tidas_package_import and exposes a TIDAS import filter label.

messages.rationale: The retained detail key is not rendered by the current task detail grid; its meaning is pinned to the active import task kind for later compatibility use.

messages.concept: Compatibility detail message stating that a TIDAS package import completed and its accepted data was imported.
messages.consequence: Must indicate completed import and must not imply that skipped Open Data records or conflicting user records were inserted.

evidence.reference: src/components/ImportTidasPackage/index.tsx payload.ok handling and import summary counters
evidence.rationale: The active success path distinguishes imported records from skipped Open Data records, user conflicts, and validation failures.

messages.rationale: The retained task-detail sentence is not currently rendered; the proposal binds it to the successful-import outcome without weakening the import summary semantics.

messages.concept: Compatibility field label for the backend jobId of a background TIDAS package task.
messages.consequence: Must label task.jobId and keep it distinct from the task object's local id, workerJobId, dataset identifiers, file name, Startdatensatz ID, and user ID.

evidence.rationale: The current task model and diagnostics expose id, workerJobId, and jobId as three separate fields (task_id, worker_job_id, and job_id).
The retained message key directly labels task.jobId in the historical TIDAS task-detail UI.

messages.rationale: The current detail grid no longer renders this retained label, but the model and historical callsite prove that its compatibility meaning is the backend Job-ID, not the task object's local Aufgaben-ID.

messages.concept: Compatibility task-kind label for an LCA calculation task shown alongside TIDAS package import and export tasks.
messages.consequence: Must denote the calculation workflow and must not imply a TIDAS package operation, a life-cycle-model entity, or a completed Ökobilanz study.

evidence.rationale: The current unified task center uses a separate active LCA calculation label for the lca task family next to TIDAS import/export filters.

messages.rationale: The retained component-level key is superseded by an active pages.process label; full-catalog review must confirm any future reuse preserves calculation rather than assessment semantics.

messages.concept: Plural summary of root records selected as starting points for a TIDAS package task.
messages.consequence: Must preserve the task's dependency-root meaning and must not describe database root users or tree UI nodes.

evidence.rationale: The active detail row confirms that rootCount belongs to a package task, while the retained plural summary is not currently rendered.

messages.rationale: The product term for root record remains BLOCKED_TERM and the exact summary placement is not active.

messages.concept: Compatibility summary for a completed TIDAS export whose generated package is identified by filename.
messages.consequence: Must preserve {filename}, indicate that package creation completed, and must not claim that the file was already downloaded.

evidence.reference: src/components/LcaTaskCenter/index.tsx taskTitle and completed TIDAS export download action
evidence.rationale: The active task card associates filename with an export task and makes download available only after completion.

messages.rationale: The retained summary is not currently rendered; the proposed context keeps its completion and filename semantics distinct from task submission and download completion.

messages.concept: Compatibility summary for a TIDAS package export task that ended in the failed state.
messages.consequence: Must identify export failure rather than import failure or download failure and must not hide a more specific available backend error.

evidence.reference: src/components/LcaTaskCenter/index.tsx packageTaskErrorText and packageBusinessDetail failure section
evidence.rationale: The active task center distinguishes package task kind, failed state, generic backend error, and the specific oversized-export error.

messages.rationale: The retained generic summary is not selected by the current task card; the proposal binds it to export-task failure while preserving specific error-detail precedence.

messages.concept: Compatibility summary for a TIDAS export package rejected because its file exceeds the configured storage upload limit.

messages.concept: Error feedback shown when an administrator attempts to add a person to the review team but no registered TianGong user account exists for the supplied email address.
messages.consequence: Must describe missing account registration and must not imply that the associated TIDAS contact is missing or that the person merely lacks review permission.

evidence.rationale: The active add-member modal queries an authentication user by email before any review role or associated Contact can be saved and has distinct handling for a missing user.
The active team-membership surface uses the same registered-account prerequisite, confirming that registration is separate from team or review-role membership.

messages.rationale: No current callsite selects this compatibility error because the active modal uses the broader user-not-found key; the account-registration meaning remains explicit for full-catalog review.

messages.concept: Field label for the localized short description of the TIDAS Contact associated with a review-team member.
messages.consequence: Must identify the Contact data set's short name and must not be confused with the TianGong account display name, email address, Contact UUID, or Contact version.

evidence.rationale: The active member modal displays the associated TIDAS Contact's localized common:shortDescription beside separate Contact ID and version fields, currently under pages.review.members.contactName.

messages.rationale: This more specific legacy label is not selected by the current component. The proposal pins it to the same TIDAS Contact field without claiming that it is an account nickname.

messages.concept: Action that searches for a registered TianGong user account by the email address entered in the add-review-member modal.
messages.consequence: Must describe an account lookup and must not imply adding the member, querying LCA data sets, or searching TIDAS Contacts directly.

evidence.rationale: The active icon button reads the email field, invokes queryAuthUserInfo, and populates user and associated Contact information without adding the person to the review team.

messages.rationale: The current control is icon-only and does not select this retained label. Its concrete account-search effect is nevertheless established for full-catalog review and any accessible future reuse.

messages.concept: Action that opens the Contact selector so an administrator can associate a TIDAS Contact data set with the queried review-team user's account.
messages.consequence: Must describe selecting an associated Contact and must not imply creating a Contact, selecting the user account, or assigning a reviewer to a review task.

evidence.rationale: The active icon control opens a Contact selector restricted to TianGong data and writes the chosen Contact reference back to the user's account before the review member can be saved.

messages.rationale: The active selector owns its icon presentation and does not reference this compatibility label. The proposal records the distinct account-to-Contact association effect for review.

messages.concept: Action in the review-progress drawer that assigns one or more additional eligible review-team members to the current review.
messages.consequence: Must describe adding assigned reviewers to the current review and must not imply creating a user, adding a review-team member, or approving the review.

evidence.rationale: The active review-progress toolbar renders SelectReviewer for the current review ID; that component selects eligible review-team members and saves reviewer assignments using a different shared action key.

messages.rationale: This retained label has no literal callsite, but the active successor control proves the current-review assignment meaning and its separation from member management.

messages.concept: Success feedback for completion of the administrator action that rejects an entire process or life-cycle-model review.
messages.consequence: Must confirm final rejection of the review and must not be confused with one assigned reviewer's rejected comment or with an automated validation failure.

evidence.rationale: The active administrator workflow invokes rejectReviewApi for the current process or life-cycle-model review beside the separate approval action; its current success feedback uses the shared component key instead of this retained module key.

messages.rationale: The key is retained without a literal production reference. The active sibling workflow establishes whole-review rejection and its feedback role, while full-catalog review must confirm the compatibility copy before future reuse.

messages.concept: Column heading for the submitting team's localized name in a table of human-review tasks.

messages.concept: Legacy selection-state label for the member unit designated as the unit group's quantitative reference.
messages.consequence: Must identify the selected member as the reference unit; a generic selection label would hide the exactly-one quantitative-reference constraint and could be confused with ordinary row selection.

evidence.rationale: The canonical English value is the generic 'Selected', while the Chinese counterpart explicitly asks whether this is the reference unit (是否基准单位); the German candidate must resolve rather than conceal that semantic conflict.
The cited TIDAS unit-group definition distinguishes group metadata, member units, and the quantitative reference unit represented by this retained control.
The active unit-group table renders each member unit's quantitativeReference field as a dedicated column, distinguishing it from ordinary table selection.
The unit-group validator accepts the unit list only when exactly one member has quantitativeReference and reports the offending member field otherwise.

messages.rationale: This retained key has no current callsite, and its English and Chinese sources disagree in specificity. The adjacent quantitativeReference label, the active member-unit column, the TIDAS unit-group model, and the exactly-one validator all converge on the reference-unit meaning; 'Als Referenzeinheit ausgewählt' preserves that meaning while remaining a state label. Approval is still required before reuse.
